MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Continued To Feb-01-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Form Interrogatories General Set One And Form Interrogatories Employment Law, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,300.75
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ALLYSSA VILLANUEVA, ESQ. of Benjamin Law Group (Tel: 510-897-9966) appearing for plaintiff;
Attys. BETHANIE E. BARNES, ESQ. and SARAH E. ROSS, ESQ. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. appearing for defendant MV Transportation, Inc (Ms. Ross appearing via CourtCall).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MV TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Form Interrogatories General Set One And Form Interrogatories Employment Law, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $7,300.75.

The Court states the tentative ruling was not contested timely. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant MV Transportation, Inc.'s motion to compel further responses to its form interrogatories - general, set one and form interrogatories - employment, set one is granted in part. Plaintiff shall provide within 14 days of service of this order verified responses (objection-free except as to privilege or work product) to interrogatories 2.2., 2.5, 2.6. 2.7, 2.8, 6.7, 8.6, and 8.7; and 203.1, 205.1, 208.1, 210.2, and 212.2. The motion is denied as to interrogatories 12.6, 200.4, and 207.2. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

The Court receives a proposed order from counsel which the Court indicates it will review.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Attys. BETHANIE E. BARNES, ESQ. and SARAH E. ROSS, ESQ. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. appearing for defendant MV Transportation, Inc (Ms. Ross appearing via CourtCall).

The Court states the tentative ruling was not contested timely. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant MV Transportation, Inc.'s motion to compel further response to its special interrogatories, set one is granted. Plaintiff shall provide within 14 days of service of this order verified responses (objection-free except as to privilege or work product) to interrogatories 2, 9, and 10. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

The Court receives a proposed order from counsel which the Court indicates it will review.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
